session_label: Mecmua / Nesîmî-Hurufi adjacency mining + candidate verification
scope: Verify 4 fragment candidates (Konya BY0000010729 / Diyarbakır Ulutürk / Ilkhchi Kırklar / AMEA Baku 2021); retrieve and parse Sohrabiabad-Akın 2019 in full as the primary citation for the two Alevi-shrine candidates; mine YEK and other sources for additional Hakîkî witnesses inside Nesîmî/Hurufi/Bektaşi/Qizilbash poetic-anthology contexts. the empirical grounding (Ankara MK A 5252) motivated the heuristic that Cihanşah-Hakîkî verse travels in Nesîmî-adjacent mecmuas.

method_summary.primary: (a) direct HTTP fetch retrieval of Sohrabiabad-Akın 2019 PDF (isamveri.org Diyanet İSAM mirror): 45-page article extracted in full; (b) authenticated YEK portal access with paginate flag for the 8 search-key probes from search_keys.json#nesimi_adjacency_keys plus disambiguating cross-checks (Yusuf Hakîkî, Hatâî+Hakîkî, Kızılbaş+Hakîkî, Hurufi); (c) direct HTTP fetch of manuscript.az AMEA acquisition page for AMEA verification; (d) post-extraction analytic step on Konya 709791 detail to position-map the Hakîkî reference within the 330-poet list relative to Nesîmî, Hatâî, Şah İsmail Bahadır.
method_summary.fallback: (a) for Iranian portals: not attempted this session (verified UK/US-IP block); (b) Süleymaniye direct catalogue: verified non-indexed in YEK in (Fatih sub-collection absent); (c) Tezkire backchain not attempted in this session.

Specific shelfmark Fatih 3808 NOT named on the page, but Süleymaniye Cihanşah Hakîkî is uniquely Fatih 3808 per the Turkey/Anatolia sweep, so identification is near-certain

results.title: Mecmû'a-i Eş'âr
results.subject: 811.202 / Şiir mecmuaları
results.notes_field_excerpt: 330-poet anthology; cataloguer: Faruk Ağartan, Kübra Ata Öğüt; ta'lik script; 150 ff., 315×225 mm. Hakîkî at position 163 in poet list; Nesîmî at 38; Şah İsmail Bahadır at 321; Hatâî at 345. Hakîkî's IMMEDIATE neighbors in the list: Mestî, Bidarî, Resmî, Hakîkî, Hevaî, Naimî, Riyazî: NOT a Hurufi/Qizilbash cluster. Yoğun nem lekeleri (heavy water damage) noted.
results.false_positive_reason: REVISED VERDICT after position-mapping: in the 330-poet list the Hakîkî sits among Mestî/Bidarî/Resmî/Hevaî/Naimî/Riyazî: i.e. generic 17th-19th-c. Ottoman tekke/Mevlevi poets, NOT next to Nesîmî (38) or Hatâî (345). The Bektaşi-tekke origin and the simultaneous presence of Hatâî+Şah İsmail+Nesîmî elsewhere in the same large mecmua does NOT individually elevate this Hakîkî to Cihanşah probability. Visual confirmation of folio range where the Hakîkî poems sit would be needed to settle, but: NOT digitized + NO author-by-author folio range in the cataloguer's note. the Turkey sweep's status was 'low confidence candidate'; the mecmûʿa sweep downgrades to 'most likely OTHER namesake (Hakîkî Mehmed Dede / Halveti / Mevlevi tradition)': keep as candidate but lower priority than originally flagged.
results.notes: Status: downgraded from 'candidate' to 'candidate but probably non-Cihanşah'. The Hurufi/Qizilbash adjacency hypothesis fails on close reading of the cataloguer's note: Hakîkî's local cluster is non-Hurufi. The entry is retained with a disambiguation tagging as 'low priority' until physical/visual access is possible. The Bektaşi-tekke provenance is a positive signal, but generic 'Hakîkî' in 19th-c.+ Bektaşi mecmuas often resolves to one of the 6+ other namesakes documented in the Turkey sweep.

results.title: Cönk
results.collection: Afyon Gedik Ahmet Paşa İl Halk Kütüphanesi
results.subject: 492.7 / Arap dili
results.notes_field_excerpt: 197 yk. cönk dated AH 1250 = 1834-35 CE. Poet list: Ahmed, Âşık Kerem, Âşık Ömer, Derviş, Fehmî, Firârî, Garîmî, Gevheri, Gubârî, Hakîkî, Hâmid, Harâbî, Kâtib-zâde, Kul Osman, Mecnûnî, Nasîbî, Nesîmî, Neşet, Niyazi, Sinâni, Salı, Sohbeti, Safâyî, Şevkî, Fikrî, Şerifi, Vâfî. Cataloguer: Mehmet Akif Alpaydın.
results.false_positive_reason: Strongly NOT Cihanşah. (1) DATE is explicit AH 1250 / 1834-35 CE: a 19th-c. cönk; (2) The poet roster is a CLASSIC 17th-19th-c. Anatolian âşık/halk-poet anthology: Gevheri (d. ~1737), Âşık Ömer (d. ~1707), Âşık Kerem (legendary 16th-c. but in folk tradition by 1834), Harâbî (Edib Harâbî, 1853-1916: date conflict), Niyazi (Misri Niyazi, d. 1694). Hakîkî in this list is overwhelmingly likely the SAME 19th-c. namesake as the Turkey sweep's YEK result 'Bayrakdar Mehmed Erzurum Janissary' or the Sinan-ı Ümmî family Hakîkî. The Hakîkî+Nesîmî adjacency here is a side-effect of a generic Anatolian âşık-tekke anthology, not a Hurufi-doctrine signal.
results.notes: NEW HIT but NOT a candidate witness. Documented 7th distinct 'Hakîkî' contextual cluster: 19th-c. Anatolian âşık / halk-poet tradition (Gevheri/Âşık Ömer milieu). Add to disambiguation roster of namesake clusters. The Edib Harâbî (1853-1916) presence creates a date paradox vs the AH 1250 = 1834-35 cataloguer date: most likely cataloguer error in dating, OR the cönk was started in 1834 and continued by later hands. Either way confirms 19th-c. provenance, not Karakoyunlu.

search_session._extra_finding: ZERO genuine new Cihanşah Hakîkî witnesses surfaced from the YEK Nesîmî-adjacency mining. The hypothesis itself is empirically valid (Ankara MK A 5252 IS a real Hurufi co-location of Cihanşah verse with Nesîmî+Yûnus Emre: established by the Turkey sweep from Anatolia DB), but YEK does NOT index that Ankara MK record (the Turkey sweep verified that A 5252 is not searchable through YEK genel_notlar despite existing in the Anatolia DB). The implication: any further YEK-based Hurufi mining for Cihanşah is likely to keep returning low-probability namesake-FP results because YEK's catalogue prose for actual Hurufi mecmuas often (a) does not list every poet inside a 300+ poet anthology, (b) when it does, the Hakîkî identification is undisambiguated, and (c) the Anatolia DB is the better hunting ground (already exhausted by the Turkey sweep). Implication: primary-source field-research bibliography (the Sohrabiabad-Akın approach) is more productive for further Cihanşah recovery than YEK adjacency mining.

data_extracted_verbatim.subject: 811.202 / Şiir mecmuaları
data_extracted_verbatim.physical_condition: Yoğun nem lekeleri mevcuttur (heavy water-damage stains)

konya_haci_bektas_by0000010729.interpretation: The Hakîkî's IMMEDIATE local neighbors are non-Hurufi, non-Qizilbash poets. The presence of Nesîmî/Hatâî/Şah İsmail elsewhere in this large 330-poet mecmua does NOT establish doctrinal-cluster adjacency at the Hakîkî text. The Bektaşi-tekke provenance IS suggestive but inconclusive without folio-range visual confirmation.

diyarbakir_uluturk_alevi_cenk.verification_method: Sohrabiabad-Akın 2019 primary-source full-text retrieval + verbatim extraction (research_log/sohrabiabad_akin_2019_witness_list.md)

data_extracted_verbatim.shelfmark: (non-institutional: held by İmam Zeynel Abidin Ocağı dedesi Abbas Önen, originally from Ulutürk/Darlı/Türkdarlı village, Bismil district, Diyarbakır; currently resident in Ankara)
data_extracted_verbatim.languages: Old Anatolian Turkish + Azerbaijani Turkish features (men, aparup, oḳı, oldı, yitdüm, virdi, idüben)
data_extracted_verbatim.hakiki_content_count: 8 ghazals: all reproduced verbatim in article appendix Ek-1

decoration_data_from_photographs.paper: cream/yellowed plain machine-made paper; some tide-line water damage; brittle/crumbling edges
decoration_data_from_photographs.ruling: simple double-rule frames in dark blue/violet ink, drawn freehand (no gilt, no zerefşân, no halkar, no decorated paper)
decoration_data_from_photographs.rubrication: absent: mahlas closures in same blue ink as text; no red rubric
decoration_data_from_photographs.binding: partly disintegrated; spine torn; covers detached or near-detached in plates

data_extracted_verbatim.field_documentation: Photographed by Bülent Akın in 2016 (two visits to Önen's Ankara residence); originals retained by Önen, photographic copies in Akın personal archive; TÜBİTAK 113K056 project (2013-2017)

cihansah_attribution_evidence.linguistic: Old Anatolian Turkish + Azerbaijani Turkish features (the article authors' principal positive evidence)
cihansah_attribution_evidence.negative_disambiguation: Yusuf Hakîkî (15th-c. Aksaray Bayrami) explicitly RULED OUT by Sohrabiabad-Akın on linguistic + tarikat grounds
cihansah_attribution_evidence.positive_doctrinal_match: Alevi/Hurufi/Qizilbash conceptual lexicon throughout (musahip, dört kapı, ikrar, mürebbî, Şâh-ı Merdân, Lahmüke lahmî): consistent with Cihanşah's known Hurufi engagement
cihansah_attribution_evidence.ritual_continuity: Konyar 1936 (Diyarbekir Yıllığı III, p.79) independently attests Hakîkî's performance in Diyarbakır Alevi cems: 80-year-prior corroboration that Hakîkî has deep liturgical tradition in this region

diyarbakir_uluturk_alevi_cenk.decoration_data_for_paper_question: The codex is 19th-c. a modest village notebook; codicological relevance to BL Or 9493's paper question is ZERO. Useful only as textual witness.

ilkhchi_kirklar_ocagi_diary.verification_status: VERIFIED: the Iran sweep/the South-Asia sweep disagreement RESOLVED
ilkhchi_kirklar_ocagi_diary.resolution_of_iran_sweep_vs_south_asia_s: Both the Iran sweep and the South-Asia sweep readings were partially correct because there are TWO DISTINCT items at this site: (1) a complete divan nüsha of Hakîkî held in Kırklar Ocağı pirs' personal archive (matches the Iran sweep's 'complete divan'); (2) excerpt cönks ('Defter / Beyaz' notebooks) used by ocak Kelâmhans for cem ritual performance (matches the South-Asia sweep's '3 ghazals': those are the 3 reproduced in Ek-2). Both should appear separately in the witness register.

data_extracted_verbatim.site: İlhıcı (Ilkhchi) town, East Azerbaijan province, Iran

item_1_full_divan.publication_status: NOT yet shared with academia; private archive
item_1_full_divan.use: Source text for cem-ritual recitation; cem-cönks are istinsah copies from this divan
item_1_full_divan.decoration_data: NONE published; codex not photographed by article authors

item_2_cem_conks.performance_context: Hakîkî Kelamlar (Hakîkî Words) performed in cem ritual; tunes called Hakîkî Makamı; outside cem performance is 'Mecazi Makam'

data_extracted_verbatim.field_documentation: Bülent Akın fieldwork in Tabriz and Ilkhchi, September 2010 + subsequent years; TÜBİTAK 113K056 project

ilkhchi_kirklar_ocagi_diary.cihansah_attribution_evidence: Same as Diyarbakır cönk: Old Anatolian Turkish + Azerbaijani features; Alevi/Qizilbash conceptual lexicon; geographic plausibility (East Azerbaijan was Cihanşah's heartland: pre-modern Tabriz was his capital)
ilkhchi_kirklar_ocagi_diary.promote_to_status: VERIFIED (was: candidate); split into TWO witness entries (full-divan + cönks)

amea_baku_2021_acquisition.verification_status: REJECTED as independent witness: confirmed Süleymaniye reproduction
amea_baku_2021_acquisition.verification_method: direct HTTP fetch of manuscript.az/index.php?17/385459.html: AMEA Manuscripts Institute new-acquisitions registry, entry #38 dated 12 January 2021
amea_baku_2021_acquisition.amea_announcement_verbatim: Azərbaycan şairi, Qaraqoyunlu dövlətinin hökmdarı Cahanşah Həqiqinin (1405-1467) 'Şeirlər toplusu – Divan'ının əlyazma surəti: İstanbulun Süleymaniyyə kitabxanası, Türkiyə
amea_baku_2021_acquisition.translation: Manuscript reproduction (əlyazma surəti) of the 'Poetry collection: Divan' of the Azerbaijani poet, Karakoyunlu state ruler Cahanşah Həqiqi (1405-1467): Süleymaniye Library, Istanbul, Turkey
amea_baku_2021_acquisition.interpretation: The word 'surəti' (Azerbaijani for 'reproduction/copy/facsimile') confirms this is a SURROGATE not an independent codex. AMEA's own announcement names Süleymaniye Library as the source institution. The specific shelfmark (Fatih 3808) is NOT named on the AMEA page, but Süleymaniye's only Cihanşah Hakîkî witness is Fatih 3808 (per Anatolia DB filiations panel of Mat 965). Therefore identification of the AMEA acquisition as = Süleymaniye Fatih 3808 surrogate is near-certain.
amea_baku_2021_acquisition.promote_to_status: REJECTED (was: candidate_likely_microfilm); now confirmed REJECTED with primary-source citation

new_witnesses_or_candidates.decoration_data_captured_so_far: None: codex not photographed in article, no description.

decoration_data_captured_for_pending_wit.summary: Sohrabiabad-Akın 2019 Ek-3 includes 7 photograph plates of the Diyarbakır Ulutürk cönk (Abbas Önen archive). The cönk is a modest 19th-c. Anatolian-Alevi village notebook: cream/yellowed plain paper, simple blue/violet hand-ruled double-line frames, dense rika script, no gilding, no rubrication, no illumination, no decorated paper. Spine torn, covers detached. Codicological value for the BL Or 9493 paper-question is ZERO; this is a textual witness only. The Ilkhchi divan and cönks are NOT photographed in the article: codicology remains UNKNOWN.
